title: Parkinson disease portfolio case-study witness (v1)
description: In-silico simulator-consistency witness for the Parkinson disease (PD) portfolio case study. HONESTY SHOWCASE: Parkinson disease is another 'CDER-but-no-clean-axis-mapping' case alongside Alzheimer (ZERO in-scope) and Type 2 Diabetes (SGLT2 inhibitors no-axis-map). Most FDA-approved PD drugs are old (1970s-1990s) CDER small molecules whose mechanisms — symptomatic dopaminergic replacement (levodopa), MAO-B inhibition (rasagiline / selegiline), COMT inhibition (entacapone / opicapone), reversible MAO-B + glutamate modulation (safinamide) — do not map cleanly onto the new expansion-layer axes. The two MAO-B IRREVERSIBLE propargylamines (rasagiline, selegiline) borderline-map onto the COVALENT axis: they DO form a covalent bond, but to the flavin N5 of FAD (not the cysteine warhead palette the COVALENT axis was registered for). They are flagged as IN-SCOPE-BORDERLINE-COVALENT with `borderline_axis_mapping: true` and explicit honest caveats; the parent covalent_inhibition_sim.py is invoked via importlib (f3 no-fork) and its Eyring TST real-limit anchor is carried forward. The other four CDER drugs (levodopa, entacapone, opicapone, safinamide) go to cder_in_scope_no_axis_mapping[] with `cder_in_scope_but_no_axis_mapping: const true` (the T2DM-portfolio honesty const). Research-stage CBER gene-therapy candidates (AAV-based, no FDA approval) are listed in not_in_scope_drugs[] as research-stage CBER UNPLACED. This is NEVER a therapeutic, clinical, motor-symptom, efficacy, immunogenic, regulatory, or portfolio-recommendation claim (g8 / f2). NOT the deferred 200-disease re-mapping; one-disease PD pilot only.

in_scope_drugs.description: FDA-approved Parkinson drugs flagged as IN-SCOPE-BORDERLINE-COVALENT. The two MAO-B irreversible propargylamines (rasagiline, selegiline) DO form a covalent bond, but to the flavin N5 of FAD (not the cysteine-warhead palette anchoring the modern COVALENT axis: ibrutinib/sotorasib/adagrasib acrylamides on Cys). They are placed under COVALENT honestly as BORDERLINE — observational covalency present, but mechanistic anchor partially mismatched. `borderline_axis_mapping: true` documents this; force-fitting without the borderline flag would be f1. The parent covalent_inhibition_sim.py is invoked via importlib (f3 no-fork) and its Eyring TST real-limit ceiling is carried forward as the row anchor.

borderline_axis_mapping.description: True — the axis mapping is BORDERLINE, not clean. The drug forms a covalent bond (observational covalency) but the warhead chemistry (propargylamine adduct to FAD N5) lies outside the cysteine-acrylamide palette that anchors the COVALENT axis (ibrutinib / sotorasib / adagrasib / afatinib). Flagging as borderline keeps the placement honest; omitting the flag would be lattice-fit-on-external-entity (f1).

borderline_reason.description: Per-row explanation of WHY this placement is borderline rather than clean.

real_limit_anchor.description: The parent sim's real-limit anchor carried forward (Eyring TST universal frequency ceiling kB*T/h for an elementary covalent step, anchoring the COVALENT axis per g1).

sim_module.description: Parent sim module imported via importlib (f3 no-fork).

sim_panel_surrogate.description: Name of the panel-class surrogate row from covalent_inhibition_sim used to exercise the kinact / Eyring TST arithmetic — NOT a fit to the drug. The parent sim's own honesty note (literature-informed warhead-class surrogates) governs.

cder_in_scope_no_axis_mapping.description: FDA-approved CDER small molecules whose mechanism does not map onto any expansion-layer axis. Distinct from `not_in_scope_drugs` (CBER UNPLACED): these drugs PASS criterion #4 drug-only/CDER but no axis fits. Schema-const `cder_in_scope_but_no_axis_mapping: true` distinguishes the category from the CBER UNPLACED bucket (same convention as case_studies/type2_diabetes_portfolio/). Four entries: levodopa (dopamine precursor amino-acid replacement therapy — not a folded peptide, not a metallodrug, no axis fit), entacapone (reversible COMT inhibitor — classical enzyme inhibition, no axis fit), opicapone (newer reversible COMT inhibitor — same class as entacapone), safinamide (reversible MAO-B inhibitor + glutamate modulator — classical reversible pharmacology, no axis fit).

fda_center.description: CDER — this category PASSES the drug-only/CDER criterion #4 (unlike the CBER UNPLACED bucket which FAILS it).

axis.description: Null — no expansion-layer axis maps onto the mechanism. The criterion #4 gate is PASSED (CDER); the axis-mapping gap is INSIDE the criterion #4 boundary, not outside.

in_scope_by_cder_discipline.description: True — the drug satisfies the CDER scope discipline (criterion #4).

no_axis_mapping.description: True — no in-repo expansion-layer axis maps onto the drug's mechanism.

cder_in_scope_but_no_axis_mapping.description: Schema-const distinguishing this honest category from CBER UNPLACED. CDER-pass + axis-tree-gap. Same convention as case_studies/type2_diabetes_portfolio/.

no_axis_map_reason.description: Per-row explanation of why this CDER small molecule does not map onto any of the expansion-layer axes (g1 honest documentation).

reported_not_run.description: Reported in the witness for honest portfolio coverage; NOT modeled (no axis sim run — no axis fits).

symptomatic_not_disease_modifying.description: These are SYMPTOMATIC palliatives — they manage motor / non-motor symptoms but do not slow neurodegeneration. Not a disease-modifying claim.

not_in_scope_drugs.description: Parkinson candidates that are HONESTLY UNPLACED — fall outside the criterion #4 drug-only/CDER scope boundary. Research-stage AAV-based gene therapy candidates (no FDA approval) are CBER-class biologics under the PHSA §351 BLA pathway. Same UNPLACED pattern as the SMA portfolio's Zolgensma row and the AXIS/HIERARCHY.tape @N genetic_medicine_status note.

fda_center_if_filed.description: CBER — the candidate class would fall under CBER review (PHSA §351 BLA) if/when filed, outside criterion #4 CDER discipline.

axis.description: Null — UNPLACED by design (CBER biologic class, no code axis).

unplaced_precedent_in_repo.description: Pointer to the in-repo precedent for honest UNPLACED handling (e.g. AXIS/HIERARCHY.tape @N genetic_medicine_status, case_studies/sma_portfolio/ Zolgensma row).

reported_not_run.description: Reported in the witness for honest portfolio coverage; NOT modeled (no axis sim run).

cross_axis_touch_point.description: Honest acknowledgement that this case study has NO clean cross-axis touch point. The two borderline-COVALENT rows do not bridge to a sister axis cleanly (the borderline placement is the whole point); the four CDER-no-axis-mapping rows have no axis to bridge from.

borderline_placement_documented.description: True — the MAO-B propargylamine rows carry explicit `borderline_axis_mapping: true` + `borderline_reason` so the placement is honest, not silent.

borderline_is_honest_not_force_fit.description: True — borderline placement with documented caveats is the honest call. Silent placement would be f1 lattice-fit-on-external-entity.
